step.text: Prepare Statement of Account and Solvency
step.name: Step 1

step.text: Prepare and file Form 11 by May 30
step.name: Step 5

step.text: File Income Tax Return by due date
step.name: Step 6

acceptedAnswer.text: Within 30 days from end of 6 months of FY = October 30 for March year-end LLPs.
Within 60 days from FY end = May 30 for March year-end LLPs.

mainEntity.name: Is LLP audit needed for annual filing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Only if turnover >₹40 lakhs OR contribution >₹25 lakhs. Below both: file Form 8 based on books maintained by partners.

mainEntity.name: What are the annual filings for an LLP?

acceptedAnswer.text: Form 8 (Statement of Account and Solvency): within 30 days of 6 months from year-end (by October 30 for March year-end). Form 11 (Annual Return): within 60 days of year-end (by May 30). Income Tax Return: by July 31 or October 31.

mainEntity.name: What is the penalty for late LLP filing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Form 8 and Form 11: ₹100 per day per form. No cap. Example: 6 months delay = ₹180 × ₹100 × 2 = ₹36,000. After 2 years of non-filing: RoC can initiate strike-off proceedings.

mainEntity.name: Is audit required for LLP annual filing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Only if turnover exceeds ₹40 lakhs OR partner contribution exceeds ₹25 lakhs. Below thresholds: prepare accounts and file statements without audit. This is LLP major compliance advantage.

mainEntity.name: Can LLP annual filing be done without CA?

acceptedAnswer.text: Form 8 and Form 11 can be self-filed. But Statement of Account requires proper accounting. If audit is required: CA must sign. We recommend professional preparation to avoid errors that attract RoC scrutiny.

mainEntity.name: What happens if LLP does not file for 2 years?

acceptedAnswer.text: RoC initiates strike-off proceedings. LLP name appears on strike-off list. Partners remain liable for debts and penalties even after strike-off. DINs/DPINs may face restrictions. File all pending returns immediately.
